dismissalText.commentary: Phil Salt c Stubbs b Kuldeep Yadav 63 (38b 4x4 3x6 54m) SR: 165.78

dismissalComment.html: Kuldeep has his revenge! It's short-ish but also wide and angled across him. Salt has to reach away from his body as he tries to flat-bat it down the ground. Doesn't get the elevation or distance and it's an easy catch for Tristan Stubbs. Two wickets in two overs, and DC may have pulled things back here

dismissalText.commentary: Virat Kohli c Nissanka b Ngidi 19 (13b 3x4 0x6 26m) SR: 146.15

dismissalComment.html: Deep point takes it, Ngidi strikes first ball!. Kohli charges at the 132ks scrambled-seam ball outside off. Was on a length and seemed to slow down off the pitch. Kohli was definitely a touch too early into the shot. Looked to caress and launch it at the same time. Didn't follow through fully, bat face opened and it went too high. Nissanka moves right and takes it

dismissalText.commentary: Rajat Patidar c †Rahul b Mukesh Kumar 8 (4b 0x4 1x6 13m) SR: 200

dismissalComment.html: Edged and taken! That was anti-climactic! Massive moment for Delhi Capitals This is a half-volley but quite wide. Patidar goes chasing with a drive and gets a thin edge off the toe end of the bat. Rahul takes a neat catch to his right. Patidar's first single-digit score of the season

dismissalText.commentary: Romario Shepherd lbw b Kuldeep Yadav 1 (4b 0x4 0x6 4m) SR: 25

dismissalComment.html: Has Kuldeep yorked Romario Shepherd? This is tossed up around leg and Romario has an odd swipe across the line and misses. Finger goes up immediately. Romario's reviewed this. UltraEdge confirms there was no bat on it. And that's about as plumb as it gets, pitching on middle and crashing into middle and off. Poor shot from Romario, and a poor review

dismissalComment.html: Mix-up and there's a run-out! Banged in short and going down leg. Krunal looked to hook but couldn't connect. dismissalText.commentary: Pathum Nissanka lbw b Kumar 1 (2b 0x4 0x6 3m) SR: 50

dismissalComment.html: Curves back in and hits front pad, big lbw appeal. Not given. Bhuvi keen, RCB review. Beaten on the flick, hit on the front knee-roll. In front of off. Looked for the most part like it'd be umpire's call, but again, no!
